title: All the way from Indonesia, she’s running the Boston Marathon for people with visual impairments
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Fransisca Miraningtyas, 46, from Jakarta, Indonesia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support people with visual impairments.
Miraningtyas, who has pterygium, underwent one eye surgery and plans another after the marathon, highlighting her personal connection to the cause.
Her participation aims to raise awareness and inspire support for affordable eye care and vision-restoring treatments.

title: 5 takeaways from the Revolution’s 6-1 dismantling of FC Cincinnati
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution tied a club record by scoring six goals in a 6-1 win against FC Cincinnati.
Brayan Ceballos scored twice from set pieces, showcasing Revolution's improvement on corner kick situations in the match.
Diego Fagundez returned to the Revolution, making his first appearance since signing as a free agent last week.

title: Man dies after kayak overturns off Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 22-year-old man died after his kayak overturned in Cape Cod waters near Wellfleet.
Emergency services, including harbormaster staff and the U.S. Coast Guard, conducted a two-hour search.
The man was found in critical condition and died at Cape Cod Hospital; his identity is withheld pending family notification.

title: Photos: Highlights from the St. Patrick’s Day Parade
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: South Boston's St. Patrick's Day Parade began at Andrew Square with Gov. Maura Healey and Mayor Michelle Wu cutting the ribbon.
Participants included Kathy Luginbuel and Teresa Rogers from Florida, marching with the Fascinators of Tampa Bay.
Festivities featured dancers from Caporales San Simon and playful interactions between spectators and parade participants.

title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Two men arrested for cocaine trafficking after a fatal overdose initiated a monthslong probe in Cape Cod.
William Sarnie of Nantucket and Andre Silvia of Wareham face charges in Barnstable District Court for drug trafficking.
Investigators linked Silvia as the main supplier; Sarnie connected via a Barnstable police investigation.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and was the musical guest on SNL's March 14, 2026 episode.
Styles performed his song 'Dance No More' from his latest album 'Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally.'
Ryan Gosling made a guest appearance at Studio 8H, introducing Styles' first musical performance.

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin, former Boston teacher, sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation.
Gavin pleaded guilty to coercion, enticement, and possession of child pornography in December 2025.
Investigators found 147 files of child sexual abuse material on Gavin's cellphone.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots considering Tyreek Hill as a free agent, despite his injury recovery and legal issues.
Marquise Brown and Jonnu Smith identified as potential free agent fits for the Patriots' offense.
Linebackers Bobby Okereke and Kenneth Murray linked to Patriots for defensive strengthening.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta led the Celtics with 24 points, 10 rebounds, and two blocks in a 111-100 win over the Wizards.
Jayson Tatum scored 20 points but continued to struggle from three-point range, hitting only 2 of 9 attempts.
Luka Garza contributed 15 points in 15 minutes, benefiting from added playing time due to Nikola Vucevic's injury.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys held a St. Patrick's Day kickoff concert at MGM Music Hall on March 13.
Opening acts included The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown, who performed alongside Dropkick Murphys.
Haywire collaborated with Dropkick Murphys on the split EP titled 'New England Forever.'
